h2h_summary: South Korea (expected Runner-up Group A) hold a narrow edge over Canada (expected Runner-up Group B) with 2 wins, 1 draw and 2 losses in 5 all-time meetings, though Canada claimed a 3-1 friendly victory in April 2026 and the two sides drew 0-0 in their only World Cup-era meeting in 2022.
team_style_home: South Korea under Hong Myung-bo deploy a flexible 4-2-3-1 or back-three system focused on quick vertical transitions, bypassing lengthy sideways possession to unleash Son Heung-min and Lee Kang-in in wide and attacking areas, with Kim Min-jae anchoring a disciplined defensive structure.
team_style_away: Canada under Jesse Marsch play an aggressive high-pressing 4-4-2 'Maplepressing' system that seeks to win the ball high up the pitch and launch rapid vertical attacks, with Alphonso Davies providing pace and directness wide and Jonathan David converting the resulting chances.
wc_history_home: South Korea have qualified for eleven consecutive World Cups since 1986 and achieved their best ever finish of fourth place as co-hosts in 2002, famously eliminating Italy and Spain before losing to Germany in the semi-finals.
wc_history_away: Canada have appeared at only three World Cups (1986, 2022, and 2026 as co-hosts), with their 2022 Qatar campaign — featuring Alphonso Davies' historic first World Cup goal — representing the high point before an early group stage exit.
group_context: This Round of 32 match at SoFi Stadium on June 28 pits the runner-up of Group A (South Korea favored second behind hosts Mexico, alongside South Africa and Czech Republic) against the runner-up of Group B (Canada as co-hosts second behind Switzerland, alongside Qatar and Bosnia and Herzegovina), with the winner advancing to the Round of 16.
strengths_home: World-class attacking combination of Son Heung-min and Lee Kang-in, both playing at elite European clubs (Tottenham/Bayer Leverkusen and PSG respectively)
Elite defensive anchor in Kim Min-jae (Bayern Munich) providing aerial dominance and leadership at the back
Tactical flexibility under Hong Myung-bo and unbeaten qualifying record across 16 matches, conceding only 8 goals
strengths_away: Electric home support as co-hosts with matches played on home soil boosting team morale and crowd advantage
Devastating pace and directness from Alphonso Davies on the left flank, Canada's all-time World Cup goalscorer
Jonathan David's prolific scoring record (39 international goals) and high-energy collective pressing system proven against top European opposition
